clinical_history: Subtle syncopation.  54-year-old morbidly obese male with an acute inferior MI.  What is the rhythm?
diagnosis: Sinus with 5:4 AV Wenckebach.  Low voltage (consistent with obesity, Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD), and/or myocardial infarction (MI) with extensive cardiac muscle necrosis) makes identification of the very broad, low amplitude P waves difficult!  Note subtle changes in T-wave morphology due to superimposed atrial activity.  (Right axis deviation here could be due to COPD, lateral extension of infarct, or left posterior fascicular block.)
